title: Inflation Surges as Effects of the Iran War Show in Prices
author: Sigma Capital
metaDescription: Inflation rose 3.3 percent in March, the highest in two years, driven by higher energy and goods prices due to disruptions from the Iran war. This spike reflect
excerpt: Inflation rose 3.3 percent in March, the highest in two years, driven by higher energy and goods prices due to disruptions from the Iran war. This spike reflects the broader economic impact of the conflict. For more details, refer to The New York Times and The Hill reports.

content.introduction: Inflation rose 3.3 percent in March, the highest in two years, driven by higher energy and goods prices due to disruptions from the Iran war. This spike reflects the broader economic impact of the conflict. For more details, refer to The New York Times and The Hill reports.
